evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/epic-bundling-unrelated-themes/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify at least one finding at major or higher severity, located in Summary or Requirements, whose body identifies that the epic bundles unrelated themes (e.g., user profile rework + billing migration + push notifications + audit logs) that have no coherent decomposition strategy tying them to a single capability, and recommends splitting into separate epics. Should NOT flag missing sections or unmeasurable criteria.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/story-mixing-feature-and-refactor/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify at least one finding at major or higher severity, located in Requirements or Summary, noting that the work item bundles two independent units of work: a user-facing CSV export feature and a large framework migration (3-4 weeks estimated), and recommending they be split into separate work items. Should NOT flag ambiguous language or missing acceptance criteria as scope issues.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/undersized-standalone-story/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify a finding at minor or suggestion severity, located in Summary or Frontmatter: type, noting that this is a trivial one-line change more appropriate for a chore or task type than a story. Confidence should be medium to reflect the judgement call. Should NOT produce critical or major findings. Should NOT flag the work item as missing required sections.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/spike-unbounded-exploration/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify at least one finding at major or higher severity, located in Acceptance Criteria or Summary, noting that the spike's research question ('understand our microservices architecture') is too broad and unbounded to complete as a single spike — it lacks a specific question, time-box, or enumerable exit criteria. This is a scope issue (unbounded exploration), which is distinct from a testability issue (unmeasurable criterion). Should NOT flag the exit criteria wording itself as unmeasurable without first flagging the underlying question as too broad.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/over-decomposed-epic/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify at least one finding at minor or higher severity, located in Requirements or Stories, noting that the epic is over-decomposed — twelve single-line rename tasks that together describe a single straightforward refactor could be delivered as one story or a handful of stories at most, not as an epic with twelve child stories. Should NOT flag missing context or unclear acceptance criteria as scope issues.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/story-crossing-bounded-contexts/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should identify at least one finding at major or higher severity, located in Requirements or Summary, noting that the work item spans three distinct service boundaries (inventory, billing, notification) and three owning teams, with no orchestration decision captured. It should recommend splitting into separate work items per service plus a coordination work item. Should NOT flag the missing ordering of operations as a dependency finding — that is the dependency lens's concern.

evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the scope lens.

Read the scope lens skill at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/well-scoped-story/work-item.md fully.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should produce no findings at critical or major severity. May produce zero or one minor/suggestion finding. The strengths list should note the coherent single-capability scope (copy-to-clipboard is a well-bounded feature). The grader rejects any finding whose body alleges the work item mixes multiple concerns, spans service boundaries, is over-decomposed, is under-decomposed, or describes an unbounded research question.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should produce zero findings at critical or major severity. At most one minor/suggestion finding total. The strengths list should note the bounded scope (three specific libraries, three-day time-box) and concrete exit criteria. The grader rejects any finding whose body mentions sizing, bundling, decomposition, or unbounded exploration.

Apply the scope lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='scope'. Should produce zero findings at critical or major severity. At most one minor/suggestion finding total. The strengths list should note the coherent single-capability epic scope (search bar) and the well-articulated decomposition strategy (three coherent child stories all serving one user-visible capability).
